cookieConsent.title: Cookie notice
cookieConsent.description: Market Twin uses essential cookies to run the service and analytics cookies to improve usability.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Market Twin (the 'Company' or 'Service') takes user privacy seriously and complies with applicable regulations including Korea's Personal Information Protection Act and GDPR. This policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and how we protect it.

collect.title: Information We Collect
collect.body: We collect the following information to provide the service:
collect.item1: Account info: email address, password hash, login metadata (handled by Supabase Auth)
collect.item2: Workspace info: company name, industry, country — entered by you
collect.item3: Simulation inputs: product name, description, price, candidate countries, competitor URLs — entered in the wizard
collect.item4: Usage logs: page visits, simulation run history, IP address (operations and security purposes)

use.title: How We Use Information
use.body: Collected information is used solely for the following purposes:
use.item1: Running AI simulations and delivering results
use.item3: Email notifications about simulation outcomes (per user settings)
use.item4: Service stability / security monitoring and abuse prevention

share.title: Third-Party Processors
share.body: We share data with the following trusted processors to operate the service. We do not sell or share user data for marketing or advertising purposes.
share.supabase: Database hosting and authentication (US / EU regions)
share.llm: AI simulation processing (simulation inputs are sent to AI models; data is not used for training)

retention.title: Retention
retention.body: We retain data until the user requests account deletion. Simulation results are retained indefinitely while a workspace is active and auto-deleted one year after a workspace is archived. Audit logs are retained for 5 years before anonymization.

rights.title: User Rights
rights.body: You have the following rights, exercisable at any time via the email at the end of this policy:

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body: We use essential cookies for authentication sessions and analytics cookies to improve usability. On first visit you can decline analytics cookies via the consent banner. Essential cookies cannot be declined as they are required for the service to function.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.body: We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email or in-service notice.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For privacy questions, please contact hello@markettwin.app.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of the AI launch simulation service provided by Market Twin (the 'Company'). By signing up or using the service, you agree to these terms.

acceptance.title: Acceptance
acceptance.body: By creating an account or using the service, you agree to these Terms and the Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, you may not use the service.

service.title: Service Description
service.body: Market Twin provides AI-driven persona simulation tools that predict pre-launch market reaction. Using government statistics and market research, we deliver country-specific demand, pricing, competition, and persona-intent analyses.
service.disclaimer: Results are AI-derived estimates intended as reference material. They do not guarantee actual market outcomes. You must use results as guidance only; final decisions and accountability remain with you.

account.title: Account
account.body: You are responsible for providing accurate information and keeping your password secure. Notify us immediately if you discover unauthorized account use. Accounts follow a one-account-per-user principle; transfer or sharing may result in suspension.

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable Use
acceptableUse.item1: Violate laws, infringe others' rights, or simulate clearly illegal products / services
acceptableUse.item2: Conduct automated load attacks, reverse engineer, or access APIs without authorization
acceptableUse.item3: Access other users' data without authorization or attempt to bypass system security
acceptableUse.item4: Resell or commercially redistribute result data without our explicit written consent

ip.title: Intellectual Property
ip.body: All code, designs, database structure, and simulation algorithms are the property of the Company or its licensors.
ip.userContent: Product information you input belongs to you, and we process it only to provide the service. You may freely use simulation results generated by the service for your own business purposes.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.body: The service is provided 'AS IS', and we make no warranties as to the accuracy, completeness, or fitness for a particular purpose of results. AI model outputs are estimates and may differ from actual market outcomes.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for any direct, indirect, or incidental damages arising from use of the service. Our aggregate liability shall not exceed the amount you paid us during the prior 12 months.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body: You may stop using the service and request account deletion at any time. We may suspend or delete accounts for terms violations, with prior notice or immediately depending on severity.

law.title: Governing Law
law.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Korea, with the Seoul Central District Court as the court of first instance. EU residents retain any additional rights granted by GDPR.

changes.title: Changes
changes.body: We may update these terms from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email or in-service notice. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For questions about these terms, please contact hello@markettwin.app.

dashboard.subtitleReturning: Project status and simulation insights at a glance

countries.title: Country Comparison

dashboard.noProjects: No projects yet. Run your first simulation.

wizard.title: Create a new project

product.title: Product
product.description: Which product are we validating for overseas expansion? Tell us the name, category, and key description.

pricing.title: Pricing & Goal
pricing.description: Set the base price and launch objective. These directly drive persona evaluation and pricing recommendations.

countries.title: Target markets
countries.description: Which overseas markets are you expanding into? Gov-statistics-grounded AI personas are auto-generated per market.

competitors.title: Competitors
competitors.description: Reference competitor URLs. Optional — leave blank if you'd rather skip.

assets.title: Creative
assets.description: Describe ad / content concepts in plain text. Adding image URLs unlocks vision-powered visual analysis for higher accuracy.

review.title: Review & Run
review.description: Final check on the inputs, then start the simulation.

placeholders.description: e.g. Active noise-canceling wireless earbuds with 8h battery, IPX5 sweat resistance. Targeting commuters and remote workers. Available in matte black, sage, ivory.
placeholders.assetDescriptions:
Examples:
Hero video — morning routine concept
Instagram carousel — minimalist still life
3-min demo video — user testimonial interviews

hints.projectName: Internal identifier. Shown on the simulation result page.
hints.description: Minimum 10 characters. Describe features, target audience, and differentiation. The longer the better — persona accuracy improves.
hints.basePrice: Current or candidate launch price. Simulation generates the curve at ±50% around this anchor.
hints.objective: Pick the #1 KPI for this launch. Persona scenarios and channel recommendations adjust accordingly.
hints.originatingCountry: Where the company is based and runs operations. Simulation evaluates candidate markets as overseas export targets relative to this origin. Default: Korea.
hints.countries: Pick at least 1 target market for overseas expansion. We recommend ≤5 to keep cost and runtime manageable.
hints.competitorUrls: One URL per line. Competitor pages help with pricing and positioning context.
hints.assetDescriptions: Describe each concept on its own line (e.g. "Hero video — morning routine"). Text-only is fine for analysis.
hints.assetUrls: Public-access image URLs (one per line). When provided, Anthropic vision inspects the actual images and lifts evaluation accuracy.

validation.description: Description must be at least 10 characters

objectiveHint.awareness: Focus on new-brand exposure and content reach
objectiveHint.conversion: Drive immediate revenue at launch — pricing-optimized
objectiveHint.retention: Strengthen repeat purchase and LTV — loyal customer focus
objectiveHint.expansion: Expanding from existing market to new countries

wizard.countriesOriginHint: 🇰🇷 The origin market (selected above) is automatically excluded from candidate targets — this tool validates overseas expansion, so adding the origin to candidates has no effect. If you want to evaluate the origin market itself, switch the origin dropdown to a different country.
wizard.assetUrlsAccuracyHint: 💡 Without image URLs, evaluation runs from text descriptions only. Add at least one publicly-accessible image URL so Anthropic vision can inspect the actual visuals — creative scores and strength/weakness analysis become noticeably more accurate.

personaCount.label: Persona count
personaCount.hint: Higher count = more accurate, but takes longer.

tier.label: Analysis depth
tier.hint: Confidence scales with the number of parallel sims. Decision and Deep aggregate N independent runs and surface consensus + variance alongside the headline recommendation.

notifyEmail.label: Email notification (optional)
notifyEmail.hint: Decision and Deep take 5+ minutes. We'll email you the result link if you enter an address.

templates.title: Start from a template
templates.description: Pick a category preset — every field gets pre-filled. Edit freely after clicking.

simulation.feelPremium: AI personas are evaluating your product
simulation.cancelConfirm: Cancel this simulation? Analysis progress so far will not be saved.
simulation.cancelFailed: Couldn't cancel the simulation. Please try again in a moment.
simulation.pollWarning: Status updates are briefly delayed — auto-retrying

failed.title: Simulation failed
failed.subtitle: Something went wrong during analysis. Please try again.
failed.retryFailed: Couldn't start the retry. Please try again in a moment.

cancelled.title: Simulation cancelled
cancelled.subtitle: This simulation was cancelled by an operator.

help.successScore: Overall launch success probability (0-100). Combines average persona purchase intent, country score, and regulatory risk into a single number.
help.bestCountry: The candidate country with the highest combined score. AI recommends prioritizing marketing resources here first.
help.bestSegment: The persona segment in the top country with the highest purchase intent (profession / age / behavior summary).
help.bestPrice: AI's recommended entry price after analyzing the pricing curve and persona price sensitivity.
help.bestCreative: The highest-scoring creative asset (only shown when creative inputs were provided in the wizard).
help.riskLevel: Overall launch risk level (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH). Combines regulatory, competitive, and supply-chain factors.

help.rank: Entry priority based on combined score and market attractiveness. Rank 1 is the most recommended market.
help.demand: Estimated market demand for this product category in this country (0-100). Reflects market size and purchase intent.
help.cac: Estimated customer acquisition cost (USD) per new customer. Reflects ad rates, channel efficiency, and competition intensity.
help.competition: Number and market-share distribution of existing competitors in the same category (0-100). Higher score = harder to enter.
help.score: Weighted final entry score across demand / CAC / competition (0-100). 100 = strongly recommended market.
help.rationale: AI's reasoning for the score. Click the row to expand into persona analysis and regulatory notes.

drill.noPersonas: No personas assigned to this country.

persona.anchoredHint: Persona generation referenced gov-statistics from the sources above.

help.intentLegend: Purchase intent (0-100) is the persona's likelihood of actually buying the product. 70+ is a strong purchase signal; below 35 is hypothetical or hesitant.

help.recommendedPrice: Optimal entry price computed from demand, persona price sensitivity, and competitor price tier. May not be the revenue-max point (margin / positioning weighted in).
help.marginEstimate: Margin commentary at the recommended price. Actual cost structure should be verified separately by the user.

why.title: Why this price
why.interpHigh: Price sensitivity skews high — lower price points convert better.
why.interpLow: Price sensitivity skews low — premium pricing is viable.
why.interpMixed: Price sensitivity is mixed — consider tiered pricing (good / better / best).
why.recommendedHint: At this price: {conv}% conversion, {rev} revenue index
why.peakHint: Revenue index of {rev} — highest point on the curve

recommendations.emptyActionPlan: No action plan was generated — try re-running the simulation.

results.pdfFailed: Couldn't generate the PDF. Please try again in a moment.

risks.linkedObjectionsHint: Persona objections sharing keywords with this risk
risks.noLinkedObjections: No persona objections directly match this risk.
risks.weakestMarketsHint: Lowest-scoring markets — exposure to this risk is likely highest here.
risks.mitigatingTrustFactorsHint: Trust factors valued by personas affected by this risk
risks.noMitigation: No trust signals registered from affected personas.
risks.anchoredHint: Personas were anchored on the gov-statistics sources above.

legend.title: Impact delta
legend.help: Delta = avg purchase intent of affected personas minus overall average. The more negative, the more this risk actually depresses conversion (rather than just being a hypothetical concern).

risks.rankHelp: Risks ranked by number of affected personas — #1 is the risk that touches the most personas in the simulation.

regulatory.title: Regulatory check
regulatory.excludedDesc: Auto-excluded from the simulation because this product is legally banned for sale in these markets
regulatory.restrictedDesc: Sale is permitted but with non-trivial constraints (license, prescription, age, regional)
regulatory.noIssues: This product can be legally sold in all candidate countries.

default.title: Default config
default.hint: Used as fallback when stage-specific envs are unset. (LLM_DEFAULT_PROVIDER / LLM_DEFAULT_MODEL)

stages.title: Per-stage configuration
stages.hint: Each stage reads its own env override first; falls back to the default config if empty.

legacy.title: Other stage data
legacy.hint: Rows whose current_stage doesn't map to a config stage above (e.g. 'failed' — recorded before the runner fix).

stageFailures.empty: No failures in the last 7 days.

providers.title: LLM Provider Health (7 days)
providers.empty: No runs in the last 7 days.

stuck.title: Stuck Simulations
stuck.description: Jobs still in 'running' state past 6 minutes — zombies left over after the Vercel function was killed. Cancel or retry them.
